ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-13-2013 Continued To Mar-27-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mercy Hall for West America Bank.

The Court noted that the parents are doing a great job in providing care. The Court also expressed concern about the Trustee's fees, the CPA fees, the parents using the childs funds to pay for his birthday party, improvement on the back yard and the basement, and the parents using funds for themselves. The Court directed Counsel to convey the above concerns to the parents. In addition, the Court reminded Counsel about the 1% guideline for Trustees fees and stated that it will not take any action on this accounting petition until proof of payment of past due assesment fees is provided. The Accounting Petition was continued to March 27,2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy A. Whitehead.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-03-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kevin Urbatsch for West America Bank.

Mr. Urbatsch addressed the amount paid to Mr. Chinskey for prepartaion of the accounting, stated that the annuity is guaranteed for 15 years or life which ever is longer, and addressed the issue of conflict with regard to representing both the Trustee of the Special Needs Trust and the Parents of the Beneficiary. In addition, Mr. Urbatsch stated that the additional expenses are for herbs, medicines, cable and other things that make the Beneficiary's life easier, and that the Bank makes the determination as to paying them. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Felicia Smith.
